pattern: Counting subarrays whose maximum lands in a band reduces to a difference of two at-most counts, each computed in one linear pass by tracking the last disqualifying position.
hints: The number of subarrays whose maximum is at most some bound is easy to count in one pass: extend a running length while elements stay under the bound and reset it when one exceeds it.
Subarrays whose maximum lies in [left, right] are exactly those with maximum at most right minus those with maximum at most left minus one.

Compute countAtMost(right) and countAtMost(left - 1) with the same linear routine and subtract; in a single fused pass, count subarrays ending at each index that contain at least one element in the band and none above right.

edge_inputs.description: mixed values, some above the band
every element above the band, answer is zero
every element below the band, answer is zero
single element exactly in the band
a tall element splitting the array into segments

stress.description: long array over a small value range with a random sub-band
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(0, 20) for _ in range(n)]
    a = rng.randrange(0, 20)
    b = rng.randrange(a, 20)
    return [nums, a, b]
stress.note: narrow value range maximises band-boundary stress
